zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland於1545年1月1日出生在蘇格蘭培爾盧維 卡素時，她的父親Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven50歲，母親Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven39歲。她於1562年5月31日與Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1627年1月1日在蘇格蘭培爾培斯享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland于1545年1月1日出生在苏格兰培爾盧維 卡素时，她的父亲Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven50岁，母亲Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven39岁。她于1562年5月31日与Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1627年1月1日在苏格兰培爾培斯享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland was born on 1 January 1545, in Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland, her father, Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven, was 50 and her mother, Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven, was 39. She married Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree on 31 May 1562, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 1 January 1627, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland est née le 1 janvier 1545, à Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree le 31 mai 1562, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1627, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland am 1. Januar 1545 in Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven 50 und ihre Mutter Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven 39. Sie hat am 31. Mai 1562 in Dunfermline, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Januar 1627 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland è nata il il 1 gennaio 1545 nel Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland, sua padre, Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree il 31 maggio 1562, nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 1 gennaio 1627, nel Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthlandが1545年1月1日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアルスベン カストルで生まれた時，父Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methvenは50歳，母Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methvenは39歳であった。1562年5月31日にイギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインでAndrew Stewart Master of Ochiltreeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1627年1月1日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland(이)가 1545년 1월 1일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 루스븐 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1562년 5월 31일 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1627년 1월 1일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.1 January 1545–1 January 1627: 1 de janeiro de 1545–1 de janeiro de 1627
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1545, em Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland, seu pai, Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree em 31 de maio de 1562, em Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1627, em Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland родилась 1 января 1545 г. в местности рутвен кастл, пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree 31 мая 1562 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 1 января 1627 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 82 лет.

es.1 January 1545–1 January 1627: 1 de enero de 1545–1 de enero de 1627
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland nació el 1 de enero de 1545, en Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland, su papá, Henry Stewart 1st Lord Stewart of Methven, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Janet Stewart of Atholl - Lady of Methven, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree el 31 de mayo de 1562, en Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 1 de enero de 1627, en Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, a la edad de 82 años.
